title: Owners of former Skyline Apartments in Syracuse hope to talk their way out of city’s lawsuit
summary: Clear Investment Group, owner of the former Skyline Apartments (now The Metropolitan), is in dispute with the City of Syracuse over building repairs and code compliance. The city filed a lawsuit Wednesday seeking a court-appointed receiver and $2 million in repairs after the developer failed to restore electricity or complete required inspections. Despite Thursday negotiations, city officials are proceeding with legal action amid deteriorating relations and ongoing housing vacancy.
department_refs: Department of Neighborhood and Business Development

political_signal.description: City code enforcement and legal action against private developer for non-compliance with repair obligations and building safety standards; indicates gap between city expectations and developer capacity/commitment on high-visibility housing project.

processing_notes: Dollar amounts extracted need clarification: $2M (requested by city), $13M (purchase price), $4.6M (promised renovations) — mechanical extraction flagged partial amounts only
Tim Knauss is author, not decision-maker; Michael Collins (Dept. Head) is primary city official quoted
